channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden says the US is 'all in' on Africa during his Angola visit meant to counter China
item.description: LUANDA, Angola (AP) — Joe Biden on Tuesday used the first visit to Angola by a U.S. president to promote billions of dollars of commitments to the sub-Saharan African nation and speak at a slavery museum, where he'll acknowledge the trafficking that once linked the nations' economies.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Pressley: Biden 'must act' on clemency before leaving office
item.description: Rep. Ayanna Pressley (D-Mass.) is renewing calls for President Biden to grant clemency to thousands of Americans serving time in federal institutions following his bombshell pardon of his son, Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Would-be Reagan assassin John Hinckley Jr. opening music store
item.description: Would-be Reagan assassin John Hinckley Jr. is opening a music store in Virginia, he announced to his followers on X.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Native American students miss school at higher rates. It only got worse during the pandemic
item.description: SAN CARLOS, Ariz. (AP) — After missing 40 days of school last year, Tommy Betom, 10, is on track this year for much better attendance. The importance of showing up has been stressed repeatedly at school — and at home.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, SHARON LURYE and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hunter Biden pardon will undermine party's 'self-proclaimed authority' on rule of law: Democratic strategist
item.description: Democratic strategist Julian Epstein says President Biden's decision to pardon his son, Hunter, undermines "Democrats' self-proclaimed authority on the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dangerous man in custody in California for murder escapes, manhunt underway
item.description: A dangerous criminal in California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation’s (CDCR) custody for murder escaped while being transported to the courthouse on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri man facing execution in the sexual assault and strangling of a 9-year-old girl
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — A Missouri man is scheduled to be put to death Tuesday evening for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl whose body was thrown into a sinkhole. Christopher Collings, 49, is set to receive a single injection of the sedative pentobarbital at 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: LAPD give update on Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities are set to update the public Monday evening with the latest details on Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old Maui resident who has been the center of a massive search since she vanished in Los Angeles in early November.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service says nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week during this year’s presidential contest.
item.dc:creator: David Sharp And Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DUI driver admits to crash that killed South Carolina bride on wedding night year after tragedy
item.description: The woman charged with killing a Charlotte bride in a crash on her wedding night was scheduled for a trial Monday, but she pleaded guilty just before it was set to start.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvey Weinstein hospitalized after 'alarming blood test,' attorney says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein was hospitalized Monday following an “alarming blood test,” his attorney said, less than a week after the disgraced movie mogul filed a legal claim alleging substandard medical care at New York City's notorious jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Coast Guard suspends its search for the crew of a capsized fishing boat in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The search for five people believed to be aboard a fishing vessel that capsized in rough seas in the Gulf of Alaska has been suspended, the Coast Guard said Monday. The search lasted nearly a day and covered more than 108 square nautical miles (370 square kilometers).
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Self-defense or too much force? That's the question for jurors as subway chokehold trial wraps
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A defense lawyer asked jurors to put themselves in frightened subway riders' shoes Monday at the trial of a Marine veteran charged with choking an irate, homeless man to death after an outburst on a New York underground train.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Komoroski sentenced for Folly Beach DUI crash that killed new bride
item.description: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) – The woman accused of drunkenly crashing into a golf cart, killing a bride, and seriously injuring members of the wedding party on Folly Beach last year was sentenced during a Monday afternoon hearing in Charleston for charges related to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Moyer, Fox 5

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: FBI offers $15K reward for Maine teenager last seen more than 2 months ago
item.description: The FBI has teamed up with Maine State Police in an effort to locate teenager Stefanie Damron, who was last seen in late September and has a history of running away.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Read this before you shop on Cyber Monday
item.description: According to Adobe Analytics, consumers are expected to spend a record 13.2 billion dollars on Cyber Monday 2024. The day was created in 2005 to promote online shopping, and it worked. Now, it's synonymous with some of the best deals of the season.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes as teen use increased
item.description: A majority of Supreme Court justices didn’t seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Sunday it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into the war-ravaged Gaza Strip because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted convoys. It blamed the breakdown of law and order in large part on Israeli policies.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: New Starbucks gives customers view of a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Customers have to pass a military checkpoint before entering the observatory where the new Starbucks has opened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, ABC 4
